event: implantation of a biventricular pacing implantable cardioverter defibrillator
found lying at home with her family
transported to our hospital by ambulance
non-contrast head CT revealed hemorrhage in the third and fourth ventricles
hemorrhage in the bilateral lateral ventricles with left dominance
source of bleeding unclear in the arterial phase
spot enhancement on the lateral wall of the anterior horn of the left lateral ventricle
blood pressure control was initiated in the intensive care unit
aneurysm (3.8 mm in size) at the distal site of the mLSA arising from the A1 segment of the left ACA
considered to be a ruptured aneurysm
no hemorrhagic complications on postoperative head CT
no cerebral infarction on postoperative head CT
Lipiodol remained as a high-density spot on the lateral ventricular wall of the anterior horn of the left lateral ventricle
decrease in muscle strength associated with disuse remained
discharged to home with a modified Rankin Scale (mRS) of 1
